_meta.title: End-to-End Clinical Data Infrastructure Protocol — TROPIC Trial (Full Rebuild)

changelog.v2.0: Full rebuild — added ADEX/ADEXSUM, ADCM, exposure-response Optimus model, subgroup forest plot TFLs, sensitivity analyses, hierarchical multiplicity control, Git/version control layer, cibuild.py dry-run/rollback, complete R Pharmaverse toolchain (metacore/metatools/xportr/logrx), corrected all v1.0 gaps

data_source.access_model: Open access. PDS Terms of Use agreement only. No IRB required under HIPAA Privacy Rule for de-identified secondary research.
data_source.primary_publication: de Bono JS et al. Lancet 2010;376(9747):1147-1154
pds_data_enrichments: MEPS linkage (RTI International) — socioeconomic augmentation layer
Prostate Cancer DREAM Challenge curation — 129 baseline clinical variables, 5 standardized raw event-level tables

regulatory_basis.adam_ig: v1.3 — mandatory for NDA/BLA/IND from 2024-03-15 (Federal Register)
regulatory_basis.submission_format: XPT v5 — currently mandatory. Dataset-JSON v1.1 under FDA evaluation (FR notice 2025-04-09); not submittable without prior FDA agreement
regulatory_basis.validation_tool: Pinnacle 21 Community (free) / Enterprise — FDA uses P21 Enterprise internally
regulatory_basis.multiplicity_guidance: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials
regulatory_basis.reviewer_guides: ADRG + SDRG required for all eCTD Module 5 packages

trial_context.design: Randomized, open-label, active-controlled, multinational (146 centres, 26 countries)
trial_context.cycle_structure: 3-week (21-day) cycles, IV infusion Day 1 of each cycle

experimental.label: CbzP
experimental.treatment: Cabazitaxel 25 mg/m² IV q3w + Prednisone 10 mg/day oral

control.label: MP
control.treatment: Mitoxantrone 12 mg/m² IV q3w + Prednisone 10 mg/day oral

secondary_confirmatory: Progression-Free Survival (PFS) — composite: radiological + PSA + pain progression or death
PSA Response Rate (≥50% decline from baseline, confirmed)
Objective Response Rate (RECIST 1.1, measurable disease subset)
secondary_exploratory: Time to Pain Progression (PPI + analgesic score criteria)
endpoints.safety: CTCAE v3.0 graded AEs, dose delays/reductions/discontinuations
endpoints.optimus_kinetic: ANC nadir, ANC recovery latency, Relative Dose Intensity (RDI) — exposure-response analysis

testing_sequence.gate: Tested only if PSA Response significant

multiplicity_control.note: All endpoints beyond Step 4 are exploratory — no alpha protected. Subgroup analyses are descriptive only, no formal multiplicity adjustment.

response_criteria.bone: PCWG3 — bone scan progression; first new lesion must be confirmed at ≥6 weeks
response_criteria.psa_progression: PCWG3 — ≥25% rise above nadir AND ≥2 ng/mL above nadir, confirmed ≥3 weeks later
response_criteria.pain: McGill-Melzack Present Pain Intensity (PPI) ≥2 OR analgesic score ≥10
response_criteria.note_ctcae: TROPIC used CTCAE v3.0. PCWG3 criteria are retrospective overlay (PCWG2 was current in 2010). Document in ADRG.

known_safety_signals.gcsf_confounder: G-CSF use is the primary confounder for ANC kinetics. ADCM G-CSF flag is mandatory before any Optimus analysis.

source_data_inventory.note: Variable names reflect DREAM Challenge standardized curation of TROPIC PDS data. Verify against your PDS data dictionary on first download — PDS may use company-specific names before SDTM mapping.

critical_sdtm_derivations.rule: min(EXSTDTC) per USUBJID — treatment start anchor for all ADY calculations
ADTC - TRTSDT + 1 (positive post-baseline); day of dose = 1
CTCAE v3.0 grade from LBSTRESN vs LBNRLO/LBNRHI. ANC: G1 <LLN–1000, G2 <1000–800, G3 <800–500, G4 <500/mm³
min(CMSTDTC) where CM.CMCAT = 'ANTI-CANCER THERAPY' and CMSTDTC > TRTSDT — new anti-cancer therapy start date for PFS censoring

population_flags.ITTFL: All randomized subjects — primary analysis set
population_flags.PPROTFL: No major protocol deviations — per-protocol set

subgroup_covariates.ECOGBL: Baseline ECOG PS (0-1 vs 2) — pre-specified subgroup
subgroup_covariates.PAINBL: Baseline pain: PPI ≥2 OR analgesic score ≥10
subgroup_covariates.PSABL: PSA at baseline (ng/mL) — continuous and categorized
subgroup_covariates.DOCPROG: Docetaxel progression timing: during vs after last dose

derivation_notes.DTHDT: From DS domain; ISO 8601 → SAS date via INPUT(DTHDTC, e8601da.)

ADEX.structure: One record per subject per exposure parameter per visit (BDS)
ADEX.standard: ADaMIG v1.3 BDS — ICH E3 exposure characterization
ADEX.purpose: Characterize cabazitaxel and mitoxantrone exposure per cycle and overall. Required for Project Optimus dose-response modeling. BSA reset flagged when weight changes ≥10% from baseline.
ADEX.bsa_note: Cabazitaxel dosed at 25 mg/m² — BSA from VS domain (weight+height). Reset BSA baseline when VS weight changes ≥10% from previous baseline per protocol. Typical BSA ~1.84 m² (population PK reference).

parameters.derivation: Running sum of PERFDOSE per subject through each cycle
Count of EX records per subject
CUMDOSE / (TRTDURD / 21) — cumulative dose divided by number of 3-week periods
Planned dose × NCYCLE × 3 / TRTDURD in weeks × 3
Count of cycles where (EXSTDTC_curr - EXSTDTC_prev) > 24 days (21-day cycle + 3-day tolerance)
Count of cycles where EXDOSE < prior cycle EXDOSE per subject
AVALC = Y if EXADJ not missing — maps to dose modification reason

parameters.PARAM: Dose Adjusted Due to AE Flag

ADEX.optimus_linkage: RDI and CUMDOSE from ADEX are the exposure inputs to the ANC nadir exposure-response model. Join ADEX (PARAMCD=RDI) to ADLB (PARAMCD=ANCNADIR) by USUBJID and cycle number for Optimus scatter plots.

ADCM.structure: One record per subject per concomitant medication (OCCDS v1.1)
ADCM.purpose: Characterize G-CSF use (primary safety confounder), new anti-cancer therapy (PFS censor date source), and prednisone compliance (protocol-mandated).

critical_flags.GCSFFL: G-CSF received Y/N — from CM where CMDECOD IN ('FILGRASTIM','PEGFILGRASTIM','LENOGRASTIM'). Flag per subject per cycle. Required stratifier for all ANC kinetic analyses.
critical_flags.GCSFPRFL: G-CSF prophylactic use Y/N — CMINDC = 'PROPHYLAXIS' within 3 days of cabazitaxel infusion
critical_flags.NACTFL: New anti-cancer therapy Y/N — CM.CMCAT = 'ANTI-CANCER THERAPY' after TRTSDT
critical_flags.NACTDT: First date of new anti-cancer therapy — min(CMSTDTC) where NACTFL=Y. This variable propagates to ADSL and feeds ADTTE PFS censoring.
critical_flags.PREDNFL: Prednisone/prednisolone received Y/N — protocol-mandated co-medication compliance check

analytic_use: Stratify ADLB ANC nadir analysis by GCSFFL — G-CSF artificially elevates post-nadir ANC, biasing Optimus kinetic estimates if not accounted for
NACTDT feeds ADTTE PFS censoring algorithm (Step 3 in PFS censoring hierarchy)
G-CSF prophylaxis rate by arm is a required safety TFL (T-15-1)

ADAE.structure: One record per subject per adverse event (OCCDS v1.1)

flags.TRTEMFL: Treatment-emergent: ASTDT >= TRTSDT AND (AENDT >= TRTSDT OR missing). CRITICAL: must be derived first before any safety summary.
flags.AEOCCFL: OCCDS v1.1 denominator flag. Y = first record of each distinct continuous episode (CIAESEQ). One Y per CIAESEQ per CQ02NAM per subject. NOT simply first.CQ02NAM.

custom_sponsor_vars.warning: Non-standard variables — register all in Define-XML v2.1 as origin=Derived with derivation algorithm. Document in ADRG Section 4.
custom_sponsor_vars.CQ02NAM: Customized Query 02 Name — MedDRA grouping of hematologic irAEs: neutropenia, febrile neutropenia, leukopenia
custom_sponsor_vars.CIAESEQ: Continuous episode sequence — increments when gap between AE episodes >3 days
custom_sponsor_vars.CIAESDT: Continuous episode start date — min(ASTDT) within CIAESEQ
custom_sponsor_vars.CIAEEDT: Continuous episode end date — max(AENDT) within CIAESEQ

episode_merging_rule.clinical_rationale: Cabazitaxel ANC nadir typically Day 8-12 of cycle; recovery by Day 18-21. AEs with gap ≤3 days represent physiological continuation of the same myelosuppression episode, not a new event. Inflating episode count distorts incidence denominators and Optimus dose-response modeling.
episode_merging_rule.aeoccfl_correction: AEOCCFL = Y for the FIRST RECORD of EACH CIAESEQ, not just first.CQ02NAM. Each new episode sequence needs its own Y denominator. This was the critical v1.0 bug.
episode_merging_rule.regulatory_basis: OCCDS v1.1 Section 4.2 — sponsor-defined episode definition; document fully in ADRG

sas_logic_pattern.note: Use RETAIN only for _ciaeseq/_ciaesdt/_ciaeedt tracking variables, not for AEOCCFL itself. Initialize AEOCCFL within each by-group branch explicitly.
sas_logic_pattern.key_step: if first.cq02nam OR astdt > (_ciaeedt + 3) then do; _ciaeseq + 1; AEOCCFL = 'Y'; ... end; else do; _ciaeedt = max(_ciaeedt, aendt); AEOCCFL = 'N'; end;

ADLB.structure: One record per subject per parameter per analysis visit (BDS)

flags.ANL01FL: Y for single best record per subject × PARAMCD × AVISITN. Resolution: (1) minimum AWDIST (closest to target day), (2) descending ATOXGR. NO RETAIN — initialize explicitly at each first.avisitn.

analysis_windows.note: Last non-missing assessment on or before first dose
Pre-cycle 2 safety gate — go/no-go for next cycle

project_optimus_derived_params.note: Derived in a second-pass data step using ADLB as input. Sponsor-defined; register in Define-XML.

ANC_NADIR.derivation: MIN(AVAL) per USUBJID per cycle where PARAMCD='ANC' AND ANL01FL='Y' AND ADY > 0 AND ADY <= (cycle_start_day + 20)

ANC_RECOVERY_DAY.PARAM: Days from ANC Nadir to Recovery (ANC ≥1.5×10³/μL)
ANC_RECOVERY_DAY.derivation: For each cycle: ANCRECDY = first post-nadir ADY where AVAL >= 1.5 AND ATOXGR in ('',.) — subtract nadir day
ANC_RECOVERY_DAY.clinical_use: Recovery latency — key for minimum cycle length optimization

PSA_DECLINE_50.derivation: AVALC = Y if PCHG <= -50 AND confirmed by second measurement ≥4 weeks later with PCHG <= -50
PSA_DECLINE_50.clinical_use: Secondary efficacy — confirmed PSA response rate

ADLB.anl01fl_bug_fix: Remove RETAIN for ANL01FL. Initialize to blank at start of each DATA step record. Use if first.avisitn then ANL01FL = 'Y'; else ANL01FL = 'N'; — no RETAIN needed or safe.

ADRS.structure: One record per subject per response parameter per visit (BDS)
ADRS.standard: ADaMIG v1.3 BDS + admiralonco ADRS PCWG3 vignette
ADRS.purpose: Tumor response analysis — ORR, BOR, PSA response, PCWG3 composite for mCRPC.

parameters.derivation: Best OVRLRESP excluding PD before first post-baseline assessment
Y if PSA ≥25% rise above nadir AND ≥2 ng/mL, confirmed
First new lesion confirmed at ≥6 weeks

ADRS.anl01fl_use: ANL01FL flags post-baseline, on-treatment assessments used in primary response analysis. ANL02FL flags sensitivity analysis excluding assessments after new anti-cancer therapy.

ADTTE.structure: One record per subject per time-to-event parameter (BDS-TTE)
ADTTE.standard: ADaMIG v1.3 + CDISC BDS TTE v1.0
ADTTE.cnsr_convention: CNSR = 0 (event), CNSR = 1 (censored) — ADaMIG v1.3 / admiral enforced convention

parameters.test: Log-rank (primary); Cox PH for HR + 95% CI
sensitivity: RMST at τ=12 months using PROC LIFETEST RMST option / R survival::rmst2()

censoring_hierarchy: 1. Last evaluable tumor assessment before PD if M2TAPD=Y (missing-to-progression)
2. NACTDT - 1 day if new anti-cancer therapy started before confirmed PD (ADCM.NACTDT)
4. LSTALVDT if no tumor assessments at all

parameters.event_definition: PPI ≥2 sustained increase from baseline OR analgesic score ≥10 sustained

parameters.PARAM: Time to First Serious Adverse Event
parameters.note: Safety endpoint — feeds Optimus benefit-risk table

project_optimus_model.framework: FDA Project Optimus final guidance (August 2024) — dose-response characterization for mCRPC re-analysis
project_optimus_model.rationale: TROPIC demonstrated 25 mg/m² cabazitaxel has 82% Grade ≥3 neutropenia. PROSELICA subsequently showed 20 mg/m² is non-inferior for OS with lower toxicity. This re-analysis uses TROPIC PDS data to retrospectively characterize the dose-ANC nadir relationship, validating the PROSELICA dose-reduction decision with individual patient-level evidence.

analysis_steps.title: Exposure Characterization
analysis_steps.description: Summarize RDI distribution by treatment arm and cycle. Identify proportion of patients with RDI <85%, 65-85%, ≥85%. Plot RDI over time by arm.
analysis_steps.tfl: T-17-1 Relative Dose Intensity Summary by Arm and Cycle

analysis_steps.title: ANC Nadir by Cycle — G-CSF Stratified
analysis_steps.description: ANC nadir (×10³/μL) per cycle, stratified by G-CSF use (GCSFFL from ADCM). Present mean, median, SD, % Grade ≥3, % Grade 4. G-CSF users and non-users must be analyzed separately — G-CSF artificially elevates post-nadir counts.
analysis_steps.tfl: T-17-2 ANC Nadir by Cycle Stratified by G-CSF Use

analysis_steps.title: Exposure-Response Scatter: RDI vs ANC Nadir
analysis_steps.description: Scatter plot of individual patient RDI (%) on X-axis vs ANC nadir on Y-axis, by treatment cycle. Fit LOWESS smoothing curve. Stratify by G-CSF. This is the primary Optimus dose-response figure.
analysis_steps.dataset: ADEX JOIN ADLB by USUBJID + cycle_number
analysis_steps.tfl: F-17-1 Exposure-Response: RDI vs ANC Nadir (Project Optimus)
analysis_steps.statistical_model: Linear regression: ANC_nadir ~ RDI + GCSFFL + AGE + ALBBL + cycle_number. Report beta coefficients + 95% CI.
analysis_steps.sas: PROC REG + PROC SGPLOT with LOESS overlay

analysis_steps.title: Recovery Latency Analysis
analysis_steps.description: ANC recovery latency (days from nadir to ANC ≥1.5×10³/μL) by cycle. Determine whether cycle delays (ADEX.NDELDOSE) correlate with prolonged recovery latency. Informs minimum cycle spacing.
analysis_steps.tfl: T-17-3 ANC Recovery Latency by Cycle and Dose Delay Status

analysis_steps.title: Benefit-Risk Table — OS vs Grade ≥3 Toxicity by Dose Category
analysis_steps.description: Categorize subjects by RDI tertile (RDIDL). For each tertile: median OS (from ADTTE), % Grade ≥3 AE (from ADAE), % febrile neutropenia. This is the core Optimus benefit-risk comparison table.
analysis_steps.dataset: ADEX JOIN ADTTE JOIN ADAE JOIN ADSL
analysis_steps.tfl: T-17-4 Benefit-Risk Summary by Relative Dose Intensity Tertile

tfl_catalogue.note: All TFL programs reference only ADaM datasets as input. No SDTM or raw data in TFL programs. ICH E3 section numbering used.

safety_tables.title: Summary of TEAEs by SOC and PT — Safety Population
Grade ≥3 TEAEs by SOC and PT — Safety Population
TEAEs Leading to Dose Reduction or Discontinuation
safety_tables.filter: SAFFL=Y AND TRTEMFL=Y AND AEACN IN ('DRUG WITHDRAWN','DOSE REDUCED')

safety_tables.title: Deaths Within 30 Days of Last Dose — Safety Population
G-CSF Use (Prophylactic and Therapeutic) by Arm and Cycle

laboratory_tables.title: Hematology Laboratory Values Over Time — Safety Population
Worst CTCAE Grade for Hematology Parameters by Arm
ANC Grade ≥3/4 Incidence by Cycle — Safety Population

exposure_tables.title: Exposure Summary: Duration, Cycles, Cumulative Dose by Arm
Dose Modifications: Reductions, Delays, Discontinuations by Arm
Relative Dose Intensity Summary by Arm

efficacy_tables.title: Overall Survival Summary (Median, 95% CI, Events/Total) — ITT
PSA Response Rate (≥50% decline, confirmed) — ITT
Objective Response Rate (RECIST 1.1, measurable disease subset) — ITT
RMST Analysis for OS at τ=12 months — ITT (Sensitivity)
Landmark Analysis: OS at 6 and 12 months — ITT (Sensitivity)

optimus_tables.title: Relative Dose Intensity Distribution by Arm and Cycle
ANC Nadir by Cycle Stratified by G-CSF Use
ANC Recovery Latency by Cycle and Dose Delay Status
Benefit-Risk Table by RDI Tertile: OS vs Grade ≥3 Toxicity

figures.title: KM Curve — OS by Arm (Primary Endpoint) — ITT
KM Curve — PFS by Arm — ITT
Forest Plot — OS Subgroup Analysis (Pre-specified) — ITT
figures.note: Descriptive only — no multiplicity adjustment. Display unstratified HR + 95% CI. Exclude subgroups with <10 subjects per arm.
figures.sas: PROC PHREG by subgroup + custom PROC SGPLOT forest macro

figures.title: Forest Plot — PFS Subgroup Analysis — ITT
Exposure-Response Scatter: RDI vs ANC Nadir (Project Optimus)

version_control_layer.tool: Git (mandatory for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance)
version_control_layer.rationale: 21 CFR Part 11 requires historical record of code changes. Git provides automated, tamper-evident versioning superior to manual SAS date-stamp comments.

commit_strategy.branch_model: main (protected) + feature branches per dataset (feature/adex, feature/adcm, etc.)
commit_strategy.commit_convention: [DATASET] [ACTION]: brief description — e.g. [ADAE] FIX: correct AEOCCFL logic for multi-sequence episodes

program_header_template.sas: /* Program: {name}.sas | Version: {n} | Author: {name} | Date: {date} | Standard: ADaMIG v1.3 | Input: {datasets} | Output: {dataset} */
program_header_template.r: # Program: {name}.R | Version: {n} | Author: {name} | Date: {date} | Standard: ADaMIG v1.3 | renv.lock hash: {hash}

version_control_layer.changelog_format: Keep a CHANGELOG.md at repo root. Format: ## [version] - YYYY-MM-DD / ### Added|Fixed|Changed entries per dataset.

package_versions.admiralonco: >=1.3.0  — oncology-specific: ADRS PCWG3, ADTTE TTE sources
package_versions.metatools: >=0.2.0  — spec-driven column ordering, CT checks, variable labels
package_versions.xportr: >=0.4.0  — CDISC-compliant XPT export with eSub checks
package_versions.logrx: >=0.3.0  — R script execution logging for audit trail
package_versions.sdtm.oak: >=0.1.0  — SDTM dataset creation from raw PDS data

workflow_sequence: 2. metacore::metacore() — load spec object from Excel/CSV spec sheet
8. logrx::log_config() + logrx::log_run() — capture full execution log for audit trail

renv_management.lockfile: renv.lock — committed to Git. Pins all package versions.
renv_management.activation: renv::activate() MUST be the first call in every R program before any library() call
renv_management.restore_command: renv::restore() — restores exact locked environment on any new machine

stage_0.name: Environment Verification
steps: python3 06_telemetry/cibuild.py --dry-run — validate environment without executing programs

stage_10.name: XPT Export with Constraint Audit
checks: Export via PROC COPY not PROC EXPORT

stage_11.name: Pinnacle 21 Validation Gate
stage_11.halt_condition: Any P21 ERROR-level finding halts pipeline. All warnings require SDRG documentation.
common_preemptive_checks: AD0018: Required variable present in all BDS datasets (STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD, PARAM, AVAL, ADT)
AD0063: CNSR = 0 (event) or positive integer (censor) in ADTTE
AD0082: ANL01FL single character Y/N no missing
AD0040: DTYPE values in controlled terminology
AD0055: AEOCCFL = Y/N in ADAE — no missing values

stage_12.name: R Independent Validation Track
stage_12.logging: logrx::log_run() wraps each program — captures session info, package versions, execution time, warnings

stage_13.name: Cross-Language Reconciliation Gate
stage_13.method: diffdf::diffdf(prod_xpt, val_xpt) for every dataset pair
stage_13.acceptance: Zero cell differences. Any discrepancy requires root-cause analysis before proceeding.

stage_14.name: Define-XML v2.1 Generation
tools: metacore + metatools (R) — preferred for programmatic generation from spec sheets
required_content: All datasets: name, label, class, structure, purpose
All variables: name, label, type, length, origin, codelist reference
Sponsor-defined vars (CIAESEQ/CIAESDT/CIAEEDT/CIAEDUR/GCSFFL/NACTDT/ANCNADIR/ANCRECDY/RDI): full derivation algorithm in Comments field
Value-level metadata: PARAMCD/PARAM decode tables for all BDS datasets
ADTTE: EVNTDESC and CNSDTDSC value-level metadata mandatory
Controlled terminology references: CDISC CT version, NCI thesaurus version

stage_15.name: TFL Generation
stage_15.rule: All TFL programs accept only ADaM XPT files as input. No SDTM, no raw data.

stage_16.name: ADRG + SDRG Completion — MANDATORY HUMAN SIGN-OFF
adrg_sections: Data source: Project Data Sphere — authorization statement
Population definitions: ITT/Safety/PP with variable derivations
Dataset descriptions: all 7 ADaM datasets and data flow diagram
Special derivations: irAE/hematologic episode merging (CIAESEQ gap rule), PCWG3 response algorithm, Optimus kinetic parameters
Sponsor-defined non-standard variables: full derivation narrative for each
Multiplicity control: hierarchical gatekeeping procedure specification
Sensitivity analyses: RMST τ=12 months, landmark at 6/12 months, ANL02FL-based PFS
Subgroup analyses: pre-specified list, exploratory-only caveat
Pinnacle 21 validation findings and sponsor justifications
Methodological caveats: CTCAE v3.0 vs v5.0; PCWG3 retrospective application; PDS comparator arm scope
sdrg_sections: Data cleaning and normalization procedures (sentinel missing values, type coercion)

execution_modes.dry_run: python3 cibuild.py --dry-run          — validate environment, check paths, verify tools; NO program execution
execution_modes.from_stage: python3 cibuild.py --from-stage 7     — restart from ADLB stage (after resolving upstream failure)
execution_modes.rollback: python3 cibuild.py --rollback         — revert 04_adam/ XPTs to last Git-tagged clean state via git checkout
execution_modes.single: python3 cibuild.py --stage adex       — run single dataset stage only

pipeline_halt_conditions: diffdf detects ≥1 cell difference in any dataset pair
XPT constraint audit finds variable name or label violations

sas: NOTE: MERGE statement has more than one
NOTE: A CASE expression has no ELSE clause
r: package .+ was built under R version

outputs: 06_telemetry/pipeline_health.json — machine-readable build report with dataset-level status
06_telemetry/health_dashboard.md  — human-readable checklist with mandatory sign-off items
06_telemetry/logrx_*.log          — R execution logs per validation program
pre_submission_checklist: [ ] All 7 ADaM datasets pass Pinnacle 21 with zero errors
[ ] All P21 warnings documented in SDRG with justification
[ ] All diffdf reports show zero differences
[ ] ADRG completed and reviewed by statistician
[ ] SDRG completed and reviewed by data manager
[ ] All sponsor-defined variables registered in Define-XML
[ ] renv.lock committed and tested on clean machine
[ ] No raw PDS data committed to Git repository
[ ] CHANGELOG.md updated with final version entry

layout.01_raw_source/: READ-ONLY. Raw PDS downloads. Never committed to Git.
layout.02_production_sas/: SAS production programs: L_staging_ingest, S_sdtm_mapping, A_adsl/adex/adcm/adae/adlb/adrs/adtte, U_xpt_export, 00_master_driver
layout.03_validation_r/: R validation programs: activate_renv.R, v_*.R per dataset
layout.04_adam/: XPT outputs: *_prod.xpt (SAS) and *_val.xpt (R). Not committed to Git.
layout.06_telemetry/: cibuild.py, pipeline_health.json, health_dashboard.md, p21_report.json, logrx logs
layout.09_tfl/: TFL programs and outputs: t_*.sas, f_*.sas, f_*.R, output/
layout.10_specs/: Dataset specs, variable metadata, SAP reference

portfolio_metadata.data_authorization: Patient-level data accessed via Project Data Sphere. Sanofi US contributed TROPIC data. Access governed by PDS Terms of Use. De-identified per HIPAA Privacy Rule. No IRB required.
methodological_caveats_for_adrg: TROPIC used CTCAE v3.0. All CTCAE gradings reflect v3.0 thresholds, not current v5.0.
PCWG3 response criteria applied retrospectively. TROPIC contemporaneously used PCWG2-era rules (2010). PCWG3 re-analysis is methodologically valid but framed as exploratory.
PDS primarily provides comparator arm data. Cabazitaxel arm availability depends on Sanofi's specific PDS contribution scope — verify on platform before assuming full treatment arm access.
Project Optimus retrospective analysis: TROPIC was not designed for dose-optimization. Findings are hypothesis-generating, consistent with PROSELICA dose-reduction rationale.
competencies_demonstrated: End-to-end CDISC pipeline: SDTM → ADaM (7 datasets) → TFL (23 outputs)
Dual-language SAS + R Pharmaverse independent validation architecture
Complete ADaMIG v1.3 compliance including OCCDS v1.1 denominator flag logic
ADEX/ADEXSUM — exposure analysis including BSA normalization and RDI
ADCM — confounder identification and NACTDT derivation for PFS censoring
Project Optimus exposure-response modeling with G-CSF stratification
PCWG3 response criteria for mCRPC (RECIST 1.1 + bone scan composite)
Hierarchical multiplicity control (ICH E9 step-down gatekeeping)
RMST sensitivity analysis and landmark analyses
Define-XML v2.1 via metacore/metatools programmatic generation
Pinnacle 21 validation gate integrated in CI/CD pipeline
Git version control for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail
cibuild.py with dry-run, stage-restart, and rollback modes
logrx R execution logging for regulatory audit trail
Working with real authorized patient-level oncology trial data
